summary: The session focused on the concepts of global full text search and form search, discussing their definitions, advantages, and appropriate use cases. The user sought guidance on designing a search functionality for a customer support team, ultimately deciding that a form search would be the most suitable option for their needs.
facts: The user is building a backoffice for a customer support team that handles customer calls. The customer support team needs to search for information such as payment methods, subscriptions, and acquired products in real time. Form search is recommended for the backoffice because it allows searching within a predefined set of fields. Form search is efficient and faster than full-text search when searching for specific information in large data sets. Full text search allows users to enter natural language queries and ranks results by relevance. Full text search can handle misspellings and synonyms, while form search is used for structured data.
